definition: A fully managed, highly available database proxy for RDS and Aurora that pools and shares database connections. Reduces application latency under bursty workloads (especially serverless Lambda) and improves failover times.
An on-demand auto-scaling configuration for Amazon Aurora. v2 scales instance capacity in fine-grained increments to meet workload demand; you pay per-second for the database capacity used.
An Aurora deployment that spans multiple AWS Regions for disaster recovery and low-latency global reads. Replicates with typical lag under 1 second; supports cross-Region failover in under a minute.

A time-ordered sequence of item-level changes (inserts, updates, deletes) in a DynamoDB table. Records are stored for 24 hours; consumed by Lambda triggers, Kinesis Data Streams, or the DynamoDB Streams API for replication, materialised views, or notifications.
A multi-Region, multi-active replication of a DynamoDB table. Provides single-digit-millisecond local reads and writes from any participating Region, with last-writer-wins conflict resolution.

DynamoDB APIs that group up to 100 operations across multiple items and tables into all-or-nothing ACID transactions. Useful when several items must be updated atomically (financial transfers, inventory reservations).
A DynamoDB billing mode that automatically scales capacity to accommodate workloads with no minimum throughput. Pay per request; ideal for unpredictable traffic and new applications.

A DynamoDB billing mode where you specify read and write capacity units per table. Combine with auto-scaling to track utilisation targets; cheaper than on-demand when traffic is predictable and steady.
A copy of an RDS / Aurora database used to offload read traffic from the primary. Async replication; can be promoted to a standalone instance for disaster recovery or cross-Region failover.

An RDS / Aurora high-availability configuration that maintains a synchronous standby replica in a different Availability Zone. Automatic failover within 60-120 seconds; transparent to application connections via the RDS endpoint.
